thesis: Closed-loop adaptive interaction for dynamic human state + context
problemStatement: Perception, biomechanics, and cognition vary across people and situations. Static XR/vehicle/wearable/robot systems can reduce safety, comfort, and performance.
approachStatement: Sense → Infer State/Intent → Adapt Feedback/Control/UI → Optimize (Safety, Comfort, Performance, Presence, Trust).
handoff1to2: Perception and attention bound usable actuation.
handoff2to3: Actuation enables task-level adaptation under safety/timing limits.

layers.name: SENSORY LAYER
layers.goal: Model multisensory perception + attention to expand the usable envelope without discomfort.
layers.keyImpact: More robust XR locomotion/reorientation and improved accessibility with preserved comfort/presence.

layers.name: MOTOR LAYER
layers.goal: Infer physical intent and deliver biomechanics-grounded wearable actuation/assistance.
layers.keyImpact: Accurate wearable haptics and intent-aware assistance for teleop, training, mobility, and XR touch.

layers.name: BEHAVIOR LAYER
layers.goal: Optimize user actions across XR, robotics, training, mobility, and driving/AV contexts under cognitive/safety constraints.
layers.keyImpact: Better task outcomes with lower risk and higher comfort across real-world, mobile, and immersive scenarios.
